categories.memoryDesc: Auto-memory and per-project conversation history under ~/.claude/projects/
categories.desktopConfigDesc: claude_desktop_config.json + config.json (MCP servers for the desktop app)
categories.noFilesFound: No files found in this category.
categories.emptyHint: Files mirrored to your backup destination for the {label} category appear here.

sidebar.triggerDebuggerTitle: Debug skill triggers and find conflicts
sidebar.masterTooltip: Files that can be saved to your master folder — CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md memory, .mcp.json servers, hooks, permissions, keybindings. Add the live source to master, restore the source from master, or diff the two.

account.title: Account
account.signedInHint: Sign in with skillsafe.ai to install, save, and share skills.

projects.title: Projects
projects.empty: No projects added yet. Click {addLabel} to pick a folder. Each project's .claude/, .codex/, .cursor/, or .agents/ directory is read when you pick the “Project” scope.

about.title: About

language.title: Language

workbench.calloutMasterOnlyBody: No live source on this machine.
workbench.calloutAbsent: Not in master yet. Add it to keep a curated copy.
workbench.calloutDriftBody: Source has changed since it was added to master.
workbench.transferToToolTitle: Translate and write this to a tool's config location
workbench.noTransferTranslator: No transfer translator yet for {category}. Open the file in master and copy by hand if needed.
workbench.primaryTitleDrifted: Capture the current source content into master
workbench.primaryTitleInMaster: Re-write the master copy from the current source
workbench.primaryTitleAdd: Save a curated copy of the source into the master folder
workbench.restoreFromMasterTitle: Overwrite the source with the curated master content
workbench.openSourceTitle: Open the source file in your default editor
workbench.transferAnotherTitle: Translate and write to another tool
workbench.noBoundSources: No sources bound. Use Transfer to push the canonical body into a tool's location and bind it as a source.
workbench.boundTitle: Bound but never synced — Restore to push the canonical body.
workbench.restoreSourceTitle: Write the master payload into this source's path.
workbench.unbindSourceTitle: Remove this source from the master entry. The destination file is not touched.
workbench.emptyStateBody: Cross-tool inventory of memory files, MCP servers, hooks, permissions, and keybindings. Pick an item from the list to inspect what's on disk and add it to your master folder.
workbench.emptyStateHint: The master folder is just a folder of files — set its location in Settings → Workbench master folder. It's safe to git init the master folder and version-control your setup.

transferDialog.titleMcp: Transfer MCP server to another tool
transferDialog.mcpNotSupported: MCP transfer to {tool} is not supported.
transferDialog.modeSectionMcp: If a server with this name exists
transferDialog.modeReplaceMemoryDesc: Overwrite the destination. A .skillsafe.bak copy is kept beside it.
transferDialog.modeReplaceMcpDesc: Overwrite the server entry if it already exists; siblings are preserved.
transferDialog.modeAppendDesc: Append to the existing destination, separated by a horizontal rule.
transferDialog.modeSkipMemoryDesc: Only write when the destination doesn't exist yet.
transferDialog.modeSkipMcpDesc: Only write when no server with this name exists yet at the destination.
transferDialog.bindTitle: Records the destination as a source on the master entry so future Restore-from-master writes here too.
transferDialog.bindLabel: Bind destination as a source of the master entry

remoteEditor.empty: Select a remote skill on the left to view.
remoteEditor.isMissingHeader: {ns}/{name} isn't reachable. A few things this could mean:
remoteEditor.missingPublishMismatch: The publish didn't actually land at this namespace — confirm in Settings that you're signed in as {ns}, then try uploading again.
remoteEditor.missingPrivate: The skill is private and the current sign-in isn't its owner. Sign in as the owner from Settings.
remoteEditor.missingDeleted: The skill was deleted on skillsafe.ai.
remoteEditor.manifestMissing: The manifest or one of its blobs is missing on the server. Try a different version from the dropdown above, or open the skill page on skillsafe.ai.
remoteEditor.unversionedHint: This skill has no published version on skillsafe.ai yet, so the file contents can't be fetched. Open the source repository to view it.
remoteEditor.binaryToast: {name} is a binary file — download to view it externally.

remoteList.missingMeta: Skill is missing namespace or version.
remoteList.quarantinedToast: Installed {ns}/{name} with quarantine: {reason}. Review before activating.
remoteList.setCurrentToast: Set current version of {ns}/{name} to v{version}.
remoteList.deleteDescription: This soft-deletes the skill on skillsafe.ai, revoking its share links and all versions:
remoteList.deleteWarning: ⚠ Existing installs will keep working, but no one will be able to install or browse this skill again.

scanReport.notScannedHint: No publisher scan report on file.

backupPanel.schedulerUnavailableWindows: Task Scheduler (schtasks.exe) was not found on this system.
backupPanel.schedulerUnavailableLinux: cron(1) was not found. Install your distro's cron package (e.g. cronie, vixie-cron) and reopen this dialog.
backupPanel.schedulerUnavailableMac: launchctl was not found. This is unusual on macOS — try restarting the app.
backupPanel.installWindowsTitle: Register a Task Scheduler job that runs the backup on the schedule below
backupPanel.installLinuxTitle: Add a cron entry that runs the backup on the schedule below
backupPanel.installMacTitle: Install a launchd job that runs the backup on the schedule below
backupPanel.daysSelected: {count, plural, one {# day selected} other {# days selected}}
backupPanel.noLogYet: No log yet. Once the schedule has run at least once, {path} will show its output here.

logIssue.tccTitle: {count, plural, one {# folder} other {# folders}} blocked by macOS permissions
logIssue.tccBody: rsync got “Operation not permitted” when opening these destinations under CloudStorage. macOS gates access per-process; folders created by an older instance of this app are unreachable to the current one until you either recreate them or grant Full Disk Access.
logIssue.fixQuick: Quick fix: from Terminal, delete each listed folder, then click “Back up now”. The running process will recreate them with the access it needs. Source files are unaffected.
logIssue.fixDurable: Durable fix: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access → add this app (and /usr/bin/rsync if you use a scheduled backup), then quit and relaunch.

backupPanel.forgetMessageIntro: The path will be removed from settings, but your existing backup files are not deleted:
backupPanel.forgetMessageWarn: Manual and scheduled backups will stop until you set a new folder.
backupPanel.uninstallMessageTail: will be stopped and removed. Your backup folder and existing files are not deleted.
backupPanel.uninstallMessageWarn: Backups will no longer run automatically until you reinstall the schedule.

status.unsupportedWindows: Task Scheduler not available on this system
status.unsupportedLinux: cron not available on this system
status.unsupportedMac: launchd not available on this system

trigger.title: Trigger debugger
trigger.hint: Type a task description to see which skills the agent would consider matching. Skills shadowed by higher-precedence ones are flagged. Below, near-duplicate skill names or descriptions are surfaced as conflicts.
trigger.queryPlaceholder: e.g. run the test suite and fix any failures
trigger.enterQuery: Type a task to see which skills would trigger.
trigger.noMatches: No skills in this view match that query.
trigger.noConflicts: No likely conflicts in this view.
trigger.conflictRowTitle: Click to filter the artifact list to just these two skills
